updates.title: Direct-GGUF qwen pack is the default bundle
updates.detail: The hosted runtime now treats qwen2.5-coder:3b as the default repo-local bundle and expects qwen2.5-coder_3b.rftmwpk in ai/runtime/chatbox/packs/.

updates.title: Oracle bucket prerequisite path is wired
updates.detail: Hosted deployments can override the pack source with ORACLE_OBJECT_PREAUTH_URL so Replit pulls the pack from Oracle Object Storage instead of the Google Drive fallback.

updates.title: Expo app scaffold now follows the hosted runtime contract
updates.detail: The mobile frontend reads runtime status, updates, research, and route maps from the lightweight Python host API instead of embedding storage or manifest assumptions.

research.title: Use Expo Router for the mobile host shell
research.detail: Expo recommends Expo Router for new projects because the file-based app directory keeps navigation, typed routes, and deep linking aligned across iOS, Android, and web.

research.title: Use pre-authenticated Oracle object URLs for pack delivery
research.detail: For a 2.13 GB pack prerequisite, a pre-authenticated object URL is the simplest hosted path because the runtime only needs signed HTTPS download access and checksum validation.

research.title: Keep the backend contract narrow
research.detail: The hosted runtime should expose a small JSON surface: health, bootstrap manifest, update notes, research notes, and route definitions that the Expo app can render directly.

routes.purpose: Show bundle health, pack metadata, and hosted runtime status.
Track runtime, pack, and deployment deltas for the hosted app.
Expose architecture notes for Oracle storage, Expo hosting, and runtime integration.
Document app routes plus the backend API routes the mobile shell consumes.

api_routes.purpose: Hosted runtime health and pack readiness.
Effective bundle contract, pack target paths, and environment overrides.
Raw manifest plus environment-adjusted bundle download settings.
Client route map and backend endpoint summary.
